titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Sean Strub Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 10 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 44 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday June 26, 2025 at 23:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of direct mail samples, printed material, correspondence, photographs, memorabilia and ephemera, writings, and computer and audiovisual media documenting the work and life of gay rights activist, publisher, and entrepreneur Sean Strub.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 59-60 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 61-63 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Professional Papers, 1982-2013, undated. II. Correspondence, 1970-2015. III. Personal Papers, 1908-2014. IV. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1993-2002, undated.

bioghist.head: Sean O'Brien Strub (1958-)
bioghist.p: Sean O'Brien Strub is an activist, author, and publisher who pioneered direct marketing and mailing techniques to fundraise on behalf of gay rights organizations and progressive Democratic politicians. He founded the direct marketing firms Metamorphics Media and Strubco in the early 1980s and formed the Strub Media Group in 1990. Strub worked mainly as a political consultant until he discovered he was HIV-positive in 1985 and turned his professional focus to AIDS activism and education. Strub was the first openly gay man to run for Congress in 1990. In 1991, he and Stephen Gendin founded Community Prescription Service (CPS), a mail-order business that filled prescriptions for FDA-approved drugs to treat HIV/AIDS and assisted patients with insurance claims; CPS also produced Infopack , a newsletter reporting on drug research and approvals. In 1994, Strub and Gendin launched POZ , a lifestyle magazine for people living with or affected by HIV/AIDS, followed by POZ en Español and MAMM , a magazine covering breast cancer. Strub was executive editor and publisher of POZ until 2004. As an activist, he was a longtime member of ACT-UP/New York, and in 1992 he produced "The Night Larry Kramer Kissed Me," a play by David Drake that addressed the AIDS crisis in New York in the 1980s. Strub published Body Counts: A Memoir of Politics, Sex, AIDS, and Survival in 2014. Publications he co-wrote include Rating America's Corporate Conscience (1987) and Cracking the Corporate Closet (1995). Strub is currently executive director of the Sero Project, which seeks to end criminal prosecution of people living with HIV. He was also briefly president and CEO of Cable Positive, a group dedicated to raising AIDS awareness on television, and served on the board of directors of the Global Network of People Living with HIV/AIDS from 2009 to 2012.

Strub was born in Iowa in 1958 to Janey and Carl Strub, and he attended Georgetown University and Columbia University. Strub and his partner, Xavier Morales, live in Milford, Pennsylvania and New York City.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Sean Strub Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of direct mail samples, printed material, correspondence, photographs, memorabilia and ephemera, writings, and computer and audiovisual media documenting the work and life of gay rights activist, publisher, and entrepreneur Sean Strub. Correspondents include Edward Albee, John Berendt, Richard Berkowitz, Michael Callen, Martin Delaney, Geraldine Ferraro, Ed Koch, Larry Kramer, Mathilde Krim, Torie Osborn, Vito Russo, Joseph Sonnabend, Peter Staley, Gerry Studds, Gore Vidal, Evelyn C. White, and Elie Wiesel, as well as Strub's business partner Stephen Gendin, CEO of Community Prescription Service and Executive Vice President of POZ Publishing. The collection contains records of Strubco and direct marketing campaigns for politicians, gay rights organizations, and HIV/AIDS activist groups; printed material, photographs, t-shirts, and other papers related to POZ , POZ en Español , MAMM , and POZ health forums; administrative and reference files for Community Prescription Service and Infopack ; and papers concerning Strub's work on HIV decriminalization. Also present are materials related to the NAMES Project AIDS Memorial Quilt, Strub's 1990 Congressional campaign, his memoir Body Counts , "The Night Larry Kramer Kissed Me," and writings of Gendin and others on HIV and AIDS.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Sean Strub Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains direct mail samples, printed material, correspondence, financial and legal records, photographs, and other papers documenting Sean Strub's business endeavors, fundraising, and activism, including direct marketing and list management, POZ magazine, and Community Prescription Service. Also included are materials concerning Strub's work against criminal prosecution of people with HIV.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Direct Marketing, POZ , Community Prescription Service, and Other Papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains direct mail samples, cardpacks, client files, and business records of Strubco and Metamorphics Media, Strub's direct marketing companies. Included are samples of direct mail for ACT-UP; the Boy Scouts of America; Broadway Cares/Equity Fights AIDS; the Democratic National Committee and the political campaigns of Bill Clinton, Elizabeth Holtzman, Edward Kennedy, Ed Koch, and Gerry Studds; Gay Men's Health Crisis; Gay and Lesbian Advocates and Defenders; Human Rights Campaign; National Gay and Lesbian Task Force; and Treatment Action Group. The subseries also contains materials related to Johnson & Johnson/Direct Access Diagnostics' Confide, the first home access HIV test, including correspondence, contracts, FDA advisory committee materials, research and focus group results, advertising and marketing files, and samples of the test kit.

did.unittitle: ACT-UP [includes stickers designed by Keith Haring]
Committee for the March on Washington
Gay and Lesbian Advocates and Defenders
Gay and Lesbian Alliance Against Defamation
Los Angeles Gay and Lesbian Community Services Center

Parents and Friends of Lesbians and Gays

HIV test kit - letters of support to Dr. Kessler
HIV test kit - pros and cons
Home access HIV testing and counseling service
Home access HIV testing and counseling background information
Mail in HIV testing and phone counseling with medical referral, briefing book
Home access HIV testing, counseling and referral service information packet for East New York HIV/AIDS Project
Focus groups - project summary and results
HIV home access test kit survey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials related to AIDS and HIV in the Black and Latino communities.

did.unittitle: Gay and Lesbian Advocates and Defenders
National Gay and Lesbian Task Force
Contracts - Anzman and Lester purchase agreement

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Community Clout was a credit card marketed to the LGBTQ community. Strub was one of the principals along with James S. Nathan.

did.unittitle: Gay marketing initiatives [of others] - direct mail

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of issues of POZ magazine, as well as administrative and financial records, correspondence, internal memoranda, notes, mockups, original artwork, photographs, reader surveys, memorabilia, and other papers related to the publication. Also included are files for POZ en Español and MAMM ; the NAMES Project AIDS Memorial Quilt; and Life Expo and Life Forum health fairs sponsored by POZ .

did.unittitle: Printing plates (Rory Kennedy, Nan Richardson Party, Smart & Strong business card)
"A Survey of HIV-Positive POZ Readers: Questions on Adherence, Dosing Regimens & Medication Preferences"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an image of a condom placed over North Carolina Senator Jesse Helms's house by Treatment Action Group.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some images are identified. Included are candid photographs of Zimbabwean AIDS activist Auxilia Chimusoro; Kerry Thomas, a board member of the Sero Project who was sentenced to prison for allegedly violating Idaho's HIV nondisclosure law; and journalist Jim Baros.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains advertising and marketing materials, correspondence, financial records, and samples of informational publications and Infopacks produced by Community Prescription Service, as well as files on AIDS service organizations, conferences, drug companies, and HIV reference and treatment. Drug company files contain press releases regarding new drug approvals, drug labels, conference materials, offprints of journal articles, correspondence, and papers related to educational grants.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Information booklets - Apples to Apples: The Guide to 2001 HIV Drug Potency
Information booklets - Double Jeopardy: The HIV/HCV Co-Infection Handbook (also in Spanish)

Living with HIV (Mercy Hospital), Miami Beach, FL

HIV/AIDS - "AIDS & HIV in the U. S.," AIDS Action Council
Patient information - Guide to NIH HIV/AIDS Information Services

Principles of Therapy of HIV Infection (NIH Panel Report Draft)
"Update on Wasting, Metabolism and Altered Body Shape"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries primarily documents Strub's work on HIV decriminalization and contains files from his tenure as CEO of Cable Positive and board member of the Global Network of People Living with HIV/AIDS. Included are reports, meeting notes, conference materials, correspondence, research, employment records, and papers concerning the Positive Justice Project, a joint project of Cable Positive and the Center for HIV Law & Policy. The subseries also contains materials related to Strub's 1990 Congressional campaign, and articles about Strub, Strubco and direct marketing, Community Prescription Service, POZ , and other topics.

did.unittitle: Sean Strub employment and enrollment package [Cable Positive]
Sean Strub's credit card receipts [Cable Positive]
"Ending and Defending Against HIV Criminalization: A Manual for Advocates," published by the Positive Justice Project
Global Network of People Living with HIV/AIDS
First Annual Gathering of Funders Concerned about AIDS
National Association of People With AIDS

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Sean Strub Correspondence and Third Party Correspondence.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence can also be found in Series I. Professional Papers and Series II. Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains personal and professional correspondence of Sean Strub and a small amount of third party correspondence. Correspondents include activists Richard Berkowitz, Michael Callen, Martin Delaney, Mathilde Krim, Torie Osborn, Vito Russo, Joseph Sonnabend, and Peter Staley; writers Edward Albee, John Berendt, Frank Rich, Gore Vidal, Evelyn C. White, and Elie Wiesel; politicians Bill Clinton, Geraldine Ferraro, Edward Kennedy, Ed Koch, Gerry Studds, and Morris K. Udall; Out magazine founder Michael Goff; and a number of POZ staff and contributors, such as Stephen Gendin, Richard Pérez-Feria, and Walter Armstrong. Family correspondence includes letters and cards to Strub from his parents Janey and Carl, sister Megan Strub Whiting, and partner Xavier Morales, as well as a 1979 letter from Strub to his parents on the one-year anniversary of his coming out to them. Other early correspondence and papers concern Strub's job with Iowa Senator Dick Clark's office.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with unidentified and first name only correspondence, cards, invitations, and announcements filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains letters, printed emails, faxes, and postcards from friends, family, and colleagues of Strub related to AIDS activism, political issues, events, fundraisers, and conferences, and POZ coverage of drug development and approaches to treating AIDS. Included are letters from POZ readers, among them prison inmates, and correspondence from Joseph Sonnabend regarding How to Survive a Plague , David France's 2016 book on AIDS activism. The subseries also contains birthday, holiday, and greeting cards, and invitations and announcements.

did.unittitle: Gates, Henry Louis, Jr. and Alvin Poussaint
Gay and Lesbian Alliance Against Defamation
Meris, Doneley and Hal (HIV Arts Network)
National Gay Task Force/National Gay and Lesbian Task Force
Sonnabend, Joseph emails to David France and Sean Strub
Strub for Congress campaign [letters of support, fundraising]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include David Hochberg, Gary Noble, Doris O'Donnell, Jim Nathan, Barton Beneš, Thomas and M. J. McKean-Reich, POZ staff, and Strub's parents and siblings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include David Cain, James Biddle, Michael Goff, William Lloyd Jorgensen, Marie C. Wilson, James Biddle, Tod Roulette, W. Paul Young, Cy Godfrey, David Hochberg, and Strub's parents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes letters to third parties from Bella Abzug, Larry Kramer, Michigan Senator Carl Levin, and Tony Valenzuela, as well as letters of recommendation for Strub.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent or folder title.

did.unittitle: Fiedler, Carol to Hattie Kaufman, "CBS This Morning" [news segment on viatical settlements]
Haring, Kay to Jeff [no last name]

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains memorabilia, awards, ephemera, address books and calendars, clippings, financial, legal, and real estate records, and writings of Sean Strub and others. Included are files related to Strub's memoir Body Counts ; works he co-wrote, such as Cracking the Corporate Closet and Rating America's Corporate Conscience ; and correspondence, reviews, and playbills for "The Night Larry Kramer Kissed Me," a play produced by Strub. The subseries also contains papers by and related to Stephen Gendin; printed email and other papers concerning Strubco and POZ ; materials related to a 2006 article by Celia Farber in Harper's on HIV; and copies of interview and event transcripts.

did.unittitle: Leanne G. Porterfield Lifetime Achievement Award
Film Alternatives 14th Annual Connecticut Gay & Lesbian Film Festival
Obituaries, eulogy, clippings, letters from Sidney Gendin to Elizabeth Taylor and Strub
Hewett, Greg - To Collect the Flesh , book and accompanying material
Article on sexual abuse and related materials (Catholic abuse scandal)
Rating America's Corporate Conscience , printed version

title.part: Transforming the Categories: A Clags Reader

did.unittitle: University of Iowa Homecoming Badges and Forgotten Traditions [inscribed by Strub]
Leases - 1 Little West 12th rider, 544 Hudson St.
Black and White Men Together of Washington, D.C. [newsletters]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles mentioning Strub as a bystander on the scene where Lennon was murdered in 1980.

did.unittitle: Bruce Decker [office files on home HIV testing and Strub]
Gay Men's Health Crisis benefit program
Body Counts and other writings by Strub
"Correctional Association of New York 2013 Comments Concerning DOH Oversight of HIV/HCV Care in New York State Prisons"
Fox, Cecil H. The AIDS Chronicles: An Apology and Treatise
Wright, Joe. Only Your Calamity: The Invention of AIDS Activism and Why its History Matters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily copies of emails and other papers printed from Strubco's AOL account, as well as notes, articles, and conference materials. Correspondents include Kyle "Hush" McDowell, Mathilde Krim, Celia Farber, Larry Kramer, Jann Wenner, and Neil Giuliano. Materials were removed from a binder labeled "SOS" during processing and rehoused in folders in their original order.

did.unittitle: Materials concerning Harper's magazine article by Celia Farber
AIDS Project Los Angeles lawsuit against Michael Anketell
Andrew Sullivan/Larry Kramer and "Cracking the Corporate Closet" transcripts

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media and Audiovisual Media.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes electronic records, DVDs, CDs, cassette tapes, and VHS tapes related to Sean Strub, AIDS activism, and POZ and MAMM .

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of computer files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by format and alphabetically by title.
